# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Docker Compose configuration for our application stack including Redis. Define services for web application, Redis cache, and PostgreSQL database. Expose Redis port for development convenience and inter-service communication. This is for our containerized development environment.
# Category: redis_exposed_port

version: '3' # Docker Compose version declaration 
services:   # Define services within this section        
    webapp_dev: &webappDev          # Reference to a named service definition    
        image: ${DOCKERHUBUSER}/python-django             :alpine          
        volumes:                      - /code/:/usr/src/app     
            appdata                   ${PWD}:/tmp                    
                redis                    ${REDIS_HOST}              # Points to a container's port where Redis server is running      
    dbpostgresdbdevsrv:                 
        image : postgres               # Provides PostgreSQL database  services    
            environment            
                    POSTGRES_PASSWORD   secretPassword          //Set up the password for your PostgreSQl in .env file and use this key as above     
                                                       ,                POSITIONAL ARGUMENT REQUIRED : set postgrespassword before running compose command                  # Set a value    For example, environment: POSTGRES_PASSWORD=secretPassword        
                    POSTMGRES__DATABASE  django-prod             //Set up database for your PostgreSQL in .env file and use this key as above      POSITIONAL ARGUMENT REQUIRED : set databasename before running compose command        # Set a value    For example, environment: POSTMGRES__DATABASE=django_dev 
                                                       ,                Both of these settings must be provided when you run docker-compose up (PostgreSQL password and database name)              .       POSITIONAL ARGUMENT REQUIRED : Set the value as mentioned above before running compose command                  # This is required only if we're not setting environment in webapp_dev service         
                                                       ,                Doing so will override all settings inside ${DOCKERHUBUSER}/.env file   If you are changing any env variable there, be sure to change here too      .  Set database-username and password before running compose command                    # Example: environment : DATABASE_HOST=postgresdbdevsrv , set it in your ${DOCKERHUBUSER}/.env file          
                                                       ;                     Postfixing with '$' inside an env block will prevent its execution if the corresponding docker service does not exist – Example: environment : DATABASE_HOST=$$POSTGRESDBDEVSRV     , Will only execute on run-time.    No effect      If no value is provided, it defaults to "default"          
            ports                    # Expose Redis port for convenience and inter service communication        
                - 6379:${REDIS_PORT}        .               REDIS             , set the redis port when running compose command by setting $(redisport) before this keyword, example : --build-arg http.proxy=http://myproxyserver              # Set a value for Redis Port
